謝拉特狂讚高貝利大帝：放在今天身價值2億

昨天（8月17日），是亨利40歲生日，曾和亨利是對手的謝拉特，送上了別出心裁的祝福。他在個人社交網站上表示：「放在今天這市場，你的身價能達到2億！生日快樂，蒂埃里-亨利！」

　　1999年，亨利以1100萬歐元的價格，加盟了阿仙奴。隨後，他在阿仙奴創造了無盡輝煌，這位法國天王力助兵工廠兩次奪得英超冠軍，三次贏得足總盃冠軍，當年阿仙奴不敗奪冠的奇蹟征程中，亨利便是領軍人物之一。在留下376次出場，228粒入球之後，亨利在2007年夏天離開阿仙奴，以2400萬歐元的身價，加盟了巴塞隆拿。隨後，他終於拿到了夢寐以求的歐冠盃冠軍。

　　在亨利生日之際，阿仙奴官方也送上了祝福：「距離不敗賽季的開幕已經過去十四年了，四十歲的你已經不是十四年前的模樣，但願十四號的你，傳奇永不落幕！亨利，生日快樂！」

Arsenal have been in contact' Everything Julian Draxler has said about transfers, Ozil & the Premier League

“Arsenal have been in contact since about two or three years ago but I was not in touch with them this winter.”

“I like English football. I like the Premier League of course – it’s one of the best leagues in the world,” Draxler told BBC Sport.

“You have big clubs like Arsenal, Manchester United and Chelsea – of course it’s a big dream to play for one of them. I have my favourite team, but at the moment I’m at Schalke. I don’t want to talk about other clubs.

Draxler is good friends with Sead Kolasinac and Mesut Ozil - and he has often spoken about playing in the same side as his German international teammate.

“He is a great player and I enjoy playing with him in the national team and I would love to play with him at PSG, Draxler told SFR Sport.

“We speak in a way like friends are talking and I say to him “come to PSG.” He is laughing and he said to me last summer “come to Arsenal.” It is just like this.

“I would love to see him here because he is a great player and an even greater guy.”

【英超預告】阿仙奴「篤」定兩連勝？

【now.com體育】阿歷斯山齊士看似必走，傳閒最近吹淡，上仗看似主場失利，最終反勝；每次阿仙奴球迷幾近心死的時候，球隊總會令大家驚豔。周六英超尾場，兵工廠主場鬥史篤城，力爭2連勝。

首輪英超，阿仙奴在酋長球場迎戰李斯特城，於83及85分鐘連入2球，以4:3反勝，拿下今季首3分，令一眾捧場客大吃驚風散。以破球會最高轉會費來投的前鋒拿卡錫迪，不足2分鐘就破關，而且整場比賽表現對辦，相信經磨合後會漸入佳境。

不過，阿仙奴在該仗的英雄，是攻入反勝一球的基奧特（Olivier Giroud），這名法國射手在陣中一直得不到應有的機會，但在上場和上季都可見，就算其踢法和整體不是perfect match，卻每次落場都能為球隊提供變招的手段，就是以高大身軀衝鋒陷陣，加強前線的身體條件；今戰出訪防線極為硬淨的史篤城，相信基奧特的作用尤其重要。

史篤城今季沒有太大變動，可說是穩定，也可說是沒有進步；說真的，他們近季的演出都差不多，就算加入了謝斯洛迪古斯和祖普莫亭，也難以改變球隊上下的安穩心態。上場，他們作客以0:1不敵愛華頓，表現也是不過不失罷了。

經過首輪比賽，未知阿仙奴領隊雲加會不會嘗試以拿卡錫迪和基奧特合作攻堅，但面對史篤城，3分應該手到拿來。

Arsenal are still interested in Monaco’s Thomas Lemar, but a move looks very unlikely now, according to David Ornstein.

Arsenal’s transfer window appears to be grinding to a halt.

After a great start bringing in Sead Kolasinac and Alexandre Lacazette, there’s been very little activity. Nobody is close to arriving and, most frustratingly, nobody seems close to leaving, either.

According to BBC journalist David Ornstein, Arsenal were still hoping to sign Monaco’s Thomas Lemar, but that hope is rapidly diminishing.

Speaking to BBC Readio 5 Live (via the Daily Star), Ornstein said: “It’s very unlikely Arsenal will sign him now.

“There is interest there but Monaco have made their stance pretty clear that he’s not for sale and it would take an incredible amount of money for Arsenal to prise him away.

“There may be one that Arsenal are possibly looking to bring in.

“I don’t think there is a specific name, but there is perhaps scope to bring in one more player.

“They’ve already spent quite heavily. Alexandre Lacazette – the record signing – and Sead Kolasinac was a free, but with their salaries that brings that spend up.

“They may look to bring a player in before the deadline but the priority is to shift a player out.”

It seems that Arsenal have put all their eggs in the Lemar basket and are now short of options during this late stage of the transfer window.

It’s possible that Arsenal will turn their attention to another position. Recent comments by Arsene Wenger about our attack suggests he thinks we might have enough there.

It’s also been reported that the club needs to sell some players before they can sign anyone else.

Arsenal have 33 players in their first team squad and are hoping to offload the likes of Kieran Gibbs, Mathieu Debuchy, Carl Jenkinson and Lucas Perez. They’ve had little luck so far.
